issues.title: Improve Header and Footer Responsiveness for Mobile Devices
issues.body:
## Description
The current header (`titlebar.tsx`) and footer (`footer.tsx`) components need improvements to ensure proper responsiveness on mobile devices. Based on review of the codebase, these components need adjustments in layout, spacing, and possibly different UI organization for smaller screens to enhance the mobile user experience.

## Why this is important
- Improves user experience across different device sizes
- Ensures consistent branding and navigation regardless of device
- Helps maintain professional appearance on all screen sizes
- Increases accessibility for users on mobile devices

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Review and update `/components/ui/titlebar.tsx` to use responsive design patterns:
  - [ ] Implement a mobile-friendly navigation menu (potentially a hamburger menu for small screens)
  - [ ] Ensure proper spacing and alignment of navigation items on mobile
  - [ ] Test display of active page indicators on mobile view
- [ ] Review and update `/components/ui/footer.tsx` for better mobile experience:
  - [ ] Update footer columns layout to stack on mobile devices
  - [ ] Adjust padding and margins for better readability on small screens
  - [ ] Ensure all interactive elements meet touch target size requirements (min 44px×44px)
- [ ] Verify that all navigation functionality works correctly on mobile devices
- [ ] Ensure no regression in desktop appearance
- [ ] Update documentation in README.md regarding responsive support

## Implementation References
- Components to modify: 
  - `/components/ui/titlebar.tsx`
  - `/components/ui/footer.tsx`
  - `/app/globals.css` (for responsive styles)

issues.title: Update GitHub Actions Workflow for CI/CD and GitHub Pages Deployment
issues.body:
## Description
We have a GitHub Actions workflow file that automatically build, lint, and tests the application on pull requests or pushes to the main branch. However, the workflow should also deploy the app to GitHub Pages, but only when changes are pushed to the main branch.

## Why this is important
- Adds onto existing quality checks, and enables continuous deployment.
- Automates the deployment process, reducing manual steps and potential errors

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Update the workflow file in `.github/workflows/ci-cd.yml`
  - [ ] **Deploy**: Deploy to GitHub Pages (only on push to main)
    - [ ] Configure GitHub Pages deployment action
    - [ ] Set up proper output directory for Next.js static export
    - [ ] Include environment configuration if needed
- [ ] Ensure proper caching of npm dependencies to speed up workflow runs
- [ ] Add status badges to the README.md file

## Implementation Notes
- We need to ensure that Next.js is properly configured for static exports in `next.config.mjs`
- May need to add a custom `export` script in `package.json`
- Consider using GitHub's `actions/cache` for dependency caching

issues.title: Add Unit Tests for Booking and Travel Guide Features
issues.body:
## Description
We need to add comprehensive unit tests for the booking and travel guide features to ensure reliability and increase test coverage across the application. Currently, several key components and functionality lack proper test coverage, which could lead to undetected issues during development.

## Why this is important
- Ensures code reliability and reduces the risk of regressions
- Helps detect bugs early in the development process
- Makes refactoring safer by verifying existing functionality is preserved
- Serves as documentation for how components should behave
- Increases overall code quality and confidence in the codebase

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add unit tests for booking-related components:
  - [ ] Create tests for `FlightBookingDetails.tsx` covering:
    - [ ] Form submission and validation
    - [ ] Passenger information management
    - [ ] Payment processing
    - [ ] Booking confirmation display
  - [ ] Create tests for `BookingDetails.tsx` covering:
    - [ ] Booking information display
    - [ ] Flight segments rendering
    - [ ] Check-in functionality
- [ ] Add unit tests for travel guide related components:
  - [ ] Enhance existing `TravelGuideMap.test.js` with additional test cases
  - [ ] Create tests for travel guide rendering in `BookingDetails.tsx`
- [ ] Add tests for shared booking functionality:
  - [ ] Test integration between `FlightBookingDetails.tsx` and `BookingService.ts`
  - [ ] Test booking retrieval in `FindBookingForm.tsx`
- [ ] Ensure all tests follow best practices:
  - [ ] Use proper mocking of dependencies
  - [ ] Include both happy path and error scenarios
  - [ ] Use descriptive test names
  - [ ] Maintain test isolation
- [ ] Achieve at least 80% code coverage for the tested components
- [ ] Document testing approach in codebase or README

## Implementation References
- Files to add tests for:
  - `/components/FlightBookingDetails.tsx`
  - `/components/BookingDetails.tsx`
  - `/lib/BookingService.ts`
  - Existing tests to enhance:
    - `/components/__tests__/TravelGuideMap.test.js`
  - New test files to create in `/components/__tests__/` folder

issues.title: Convert all test files from JavaScript to TypeScript
issues.body:
## Description

Our codebase currently uses a mix of JavaScript (.js/.jsx) and TypeScript (.ts/.tsx) for test files. To maintain code consistency, improve type safety, and align with our project guidelines which specify using TypeScript interfaces/types for all props and data structures, we should convert all JavaScript test files to TypeScript.

Converting tests to TypeScript will provide better type checking, improved auto-completion, and make our test suite more maintainable. This will also ensure consistency across our codebase and help prevent type-related bugs in our tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Convert the following test files from JavaScript to TypeScript:
  - [ ] `lib/flightDataCache.test.js` → `.test.ts`
  - [ ] `lib/BookingService.test.js` → `.test.ts`
  - [ ] `components/__tests__/FindBookingForm.test.js` → `.test.tsx`
  - [ ] `components/__tests__/pointsActivityTable.test.js` → `.test.tsx`
  - [ ] `components/__tests__/travelGuideForm.test.js` → `.test.tsx`
  - [ ] `components/__tests__/TravelGuideMap.test.js` → `.test.tsx`
  - [ ] `app/admin/travelguide/page.test.jsx` → `.test.tsx`

- [ ] Add proper type declarations for:
  - [ ] Test functions and parameters
  - [ ] Mock objects and functions
  - [ ] Component props in component tests
  - [ ] Expected test results and assertions

- [ ] Ensure all converted tests pass by running `npm test` for each converted file
- [ ] Update any imports as needed to follow TypeScript conventions
- [ ] Ensure the overall test coverage remains the same or improves

## Implementation Notes

1. **General approach:**
   - Create TypeScript versions of each file
   - Add appropriate type annotations
   - Update any imports or code as needed
   - Verify tests pass

2. **Special considerations:**
   - Some test files (like `flightDataCache.test.js`) use Jest's `jsdom` environment. Make sure to maintain the environment comment at the top of the file.
   - For React component tests, use the `.test.tsx` extension
   - For service/utility tests, use the `.test.ts` extension

3. **Testing:**
   - After conversion, run `npm test` on the converted files to verify functionality
   - Check that test coverage metrics haven't decreased

## Why This Matters

Consistency is one of our key code standards, and having a mix of JavaScript and TypeScript test files makes the codebase harder to maintain. TypeScript will also help catch potential issues in our tests earlier and provide better IDE integration for test development.

## Related Files

- `jest.config.js`
- `jest.setup.js`

issues.title: Improve Validation for Payment Information in Booking Process
issues.body:
## Description
The current payment/confirmation process in the flight booking flow lacks comprehensive validation for credit card information and other payment details. This could lead to invalid data being submitted or confusing error messages for users. We need to implement robust validation to ensure users enter valid payment information and receive helpful feedback when errors occur.

## Why this is important
- Prevents invalid data from being submitted in the payment process
- Improves user experience by providing immediate feedback on input errors
- Reduces potential issues with payment processing
- Increases user confidence in the security and reliability of the booking system
- Aligns with industry best practices for payment form validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Enhance validation in `FlightBookingDetails.tsx` for the payment form:
  - [ ] Add credit card number validation using Luhn algorithm and card type detection
  - [ ] Implement expiry date validation (must be a future date in MM/YY format)
  - [ ] Add CVV validation (3-4 digits depending on card type)
  - [ ] Validate cardholder name (required, proper format)
- [ ] Provide real-time feedback as users type:
  - [ ] Show validation status (success/error) with appropriate styling
  - [ ] Display specific, helpful error messages for each field
  - [ ] Format credit card number automatically (add spaces every 4 digits)
  - [ ] Format expiry date automatically (add slash after month)
- [ ] Improve error handling in the form submission process:
  - [ ] Prevent submission when validation fails
  - [ ] Highlight all fields with errors
  - [ ] Focus the first field with error
  - [ ] Show a summary of errors when appropriate
- [ ] Add accessibility features:
  - [ ] ARIA attributes for error messages
  - [ ] Keyboard navigation support
  - [ ] Screen reader friendly error announcements
- [ ] Write unit tests for the validation functions
- [ ] Ensure the validation doesn't interfere with the "completed booking" view

## Implementation References
- Files to modify:
  - `/components/FlightBookingDetails.tsx` (primary file for modification)
  - Create validation utility functions if needed
  - Add tests for new validation logic

issues.title: Refactor FindYourTripForm into Smaller, Reusable Components
issues.body:
## Description
The `FindYourTripForm.tsx` component is currently large and complex (approximately 900 lines of code), handling multiple responsibilities including trip type selection, form field rendering, search functionality, and results display. We need to refactor this into smaller, reusable components to improve code maintainability and readability.

## Why this is important
- Improves code maintainability by breaking down a complex component
- Makes the codebase easier to understand for new developers
- Facilitates independent testing of component parts
- Allows easier feature additions in the future
- Follows React best practices of component composition

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Break down `FindYourTripForm.tsx` into logical, smaller components:
  - [ ] Create a `TripTypeSelector` component for handling trip type selection
  - [ ] Create form field components (e.g., `AirportSelector`, `DateSelector`, `PassengerSelector`)
  - [ ] Create a `FlightSearchResults` component to display search results
  - [ ] Create a `CollapsedSearchForm` component for the minimized search form view
- [ ] Update imports and integrations in dependent files like `app/page.tsx` and `app/search/page.tsx`
- [ ] Ensure all component props are properly typed with TypeScript
- [ ] Maintain all existing functionality during refactoring
- [ ] Implement proper state management between components (consider Context API if needed)
- [ ] Add unit tests for each new component
- [ ] Document the component structure in a README or comments
- [ ] Ensure responsive design is maintained throughout refactoring

## Implementation References
- Files to modify:
  - `/components/FindYourTripForm.tsx` (primary file to refactor)
  - Create new component files for each extracted component
  - Update `/app/page.tsx` and `/app/search/page.tsx` for integration
- Consider refactoring shared types into separate files as needed

issues.title: Refactor similarly named components and remove unused files
issues.body:
## Overview

This issue addresses code organization concerns in the Copilot Airways application. We've identified several similarly named components that serve different purposes but have confusing naming conventions. Additionally, there may be unused files that should be removed to improve maintainability. Addressing these issues will make the codebase more intuitive, reduce confusion for developers, and improve overall code quality.

## Problem Description

After reviewing the codebase, we've identified:

1. **Similarly Named Components**:
   - `BookingDetails.tsx` vs `FlightBookingDetails.tsx` - Both handle booking details but in different contexts
   - `FindBookingForm.tsx` vs `FindYourTripForm.tsx` - Both are search forms but for completely different purposes
   - `travelGuideForm.tsx` vs `TravelGuideMap.tsx` - Inconsistent capitalization (lowercase 't' vs uppercase 'T')

2. **Potential Unused Files**:
   - Need to thoroughly validate which files are not used or linked in the application

This situation creates confusion during development, makes it harder for new developers to understand the codebase, and can lead to potential errors when modifying related functionality.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Rename `FindYourTripForm.tsx` to `FlightSearchForm.tsx` to better reflect its purpose (searching for flights)
- [ ] Rename `FindBookingForm.tsx` to `RetrieveBookingForm.tsx` to clarify its purpose (retrieving existing bookings)
- [ ] Standardize component naming by renaming `travelGuideForm.tsx` to `TravelGuideForm.tsx` for consistent PascalCase
- [ ] Add descriptive documentation comments at the top of each renamed file explaining its purpose
- [ ] Update all imports and references to these components throughout the codebase
- [ ] Update test files to reflect new component names
- [ ] Identify any unused files in the codebase and mark them for removal
- [ ] Update relevant section in README to clarify component naming patterns
- [ ] Ensure all tests pass after renaming

## Implementation Details

Files that need to be modified:
- `components/FindYourTripForm.tsx` → `FlightSearchForm.tsx`
- `components/FindBookingForm.tsx` → `RetrieveBookingForm.tsx`
- `components/travelGuideForm.tsx` → `TravelGuideForm.tsx`

Additional files that will need import updates:
- `app/page.tsx` (likely uses FindYourTripForm)
- `app/find-booking/page.tsx` (likely uses FindBookingForm)
- `app/admin/travelguide/page.tsx` (likely uses travelGuideForm)
- Any test files referencing these components

## Additional Context

- The `BookingDetails.tsx` and `FlightBookingDetails.tsx` components should remain as separate files since they serve different purposes (showing existing bookings vs the flight booking process), but we should ensure their documentation clearly distinguishes between them
- We should update documentation to clarify:
  - `BookingDetails.tsx` - Displays details of an existing booking
  - `FlightBookingDetails.tsx` - Handles the flight booking process with passenger information and payment

This refactoring will make the codebase more intuitive and easier to maintain, especially as new features are added.

issues.title: Enhance Accessibility for Images and Forms
issues.body:
## Description
The application needs improved accessibility for images and forms to meet accessibility standards and provide a better experience for all users, including those using assistive technologies. Currently, many images may be missing proper alt text, and forms might not have appropriate labels, ARIA attributes, or keyboard navigation support.

## Why this is important
- Ensures the application is usable by people with various disabilities
- Complies with accessibility standards such as WCAG 2.1
- Improves the overall user experience for all users
- Reduces legal risks associated with inaccessible web applications
- Demonstrates our commitment to inclusive design

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Enhance image accessibility:
  - [ ] Add meaningful alt text to all images in the application
  - [ ] Provide empty alt attributes (alt="") for decorative images
  - [ ] Ensure SVG images have appropriate ARIA labels or titles
  - [ ] Review image contrast ratios against backgrounds
- [ ] Improve form accessibility:
  - [ ] Ensure all form inputs have associated labels
  - [ ] Add aria-required attributes to required fields
  - [ ] Include aria-describedby for fields with additional instructions
  - [ ] Add proper error messaging with aria-invalid states
  - [ ] Ensure tab order is logical and keyboard navigation works correctly
- [ ] Focus on these key components:
  - [ ] `FindYourTripForm.tsx`
  - [ ] `FlightBookingDetails.tsx`
  - [ ] `FindBookingForm.tsx`
  - [ ] `TravelGuideForm.tsx`
- [ ] Test with screen readers (NVDA, VoiceOver) to verify improvements
- [ ] Run automated accessibility checks using tools like axe or Lighthouse
- [ ] Document accessibility improvements in the README.md
- [ ] Ensure all changes meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards at minimum

## Implementation References
- Files to modify:
  - All components with images or form elements, with particular focus on:
    - `/components/FindYourTripForm.tsx`
    - `/components/FlightBookingDetails.tsx`
    - `/components/FindBookingForm.tsx`
    - `/components/TravelGuideMap.tsx`
    - `/components/travelGuideForm.tsx`
    - `/components/ui/titlebar.tsx`
    - `/components/ui/footer.tsx`
